fact: March 3 is exactly 62 days into the year, which means there are 303 days left - that's triple threes!
In the Northern Hemisphere, March 3 sits smack in the middle of when maple trees start making their sweet, sticky sap that becomes syrup!
Did you know that March 3 is when many hibernating animals like groundhogs and bears start getting seriously restless in their dens?
The sun rises about 2 minutes earlier each day around March 3 in most places, so daylight is growing super fast!
March 3 falls right in Pisces season, making it one of the most dreamy and imaginative days of the zodiac calendar.
In Australia and other Southern Hemisphere places, March 3 is when autumn really kicks in and leaves start their colorful transformation!
This date is exactly 18 days before the spring equinox, when day and night become perfectly equal lengths.
March 3 is prime time for seeing the constellation Orion the Hunter blazing bright in the evening sky!
In cold northern places, March 3 is when ice on lakes and ponds starts making weird creaking and groaning sounds as it begins to crack.
Many types of frogs and toads start waking up from their winter sleep around March 3, getting ready to make their loud spring songs!
The average temperature difference between March 3 and March 31 is about 15 degrees Fahrenheit in most temperate zones - that's a huge jump in just one month!
March 3 is when many flowers like crocuses and snowdrops are pushing up through the last bits of snow in gardens across Europe and North America.
